briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines rather than actual artificial intelligence.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting critical risks in the bank-fintech partnership model.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving a new wave of unconventional credit card products, reshaping how consumers access and use credit.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial services and credit decisioning.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — An in-depth analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada formally flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a foundational moment in the country's ongoing financial data reform journey.

- **[CVCA and NACO offer competing visions for feds' $750-million venture envelope](https://betakit.com/cvca-and-naco-offer-competing-visions-for-feds-750-million-venture-envelope/)** — *BetaKit* — Canada's two leading venture capital associations are presenting divergent proposals to the federal government on how to deploy a $750-million venture funding envelope, with significant implications for fintech and tech startups nationwide.

- **[UniUni raises $85 million USD as it scales gig-powered delivery across North America](https://betakit.com/uniuni-raises-85-million-usd-as-it-scales-gig-powered-delivery-across-north-america/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian last-mile delivery startup UniUni has raised $85 million USD to expand its gig-economy-powered delivery network across North America, reflecting continued investor appetite for Canadian tech scale-ups.

- **[How Gaiia used its R&D to help finance a pivot from services to software](https://betakit.com/how-gaiia-used-its-rd-to-help-finance-a-pivot-from-services-to-software/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian startup Gaiia shares how it leveraged R&D financing strategies to successfully transition its business model from services to software, offering lessons for Atlantic Canadian founders.

- **[Trump wants AI data centres to bring their own power. Alberta's been doing that from the get-go](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/calgary/data-centre-buildout-alberta-us-9.7111850?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Alberta's early adoption of self-powered data centre infrastructure positions Canada as a model for the kind of AI-ready digital infrastructure that underpins fintech and financial services innovation.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A provocative resource cataloguing the ways fintech products can harm consumers, offering important cautionary lessons for ecosystem builders focused on financial inclusion and responsible innovation in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Canada's auto market is officially open to Chinese EVs, but you won't see cheaper models right away](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/chinese-evs-canada-timeline-9.7117780?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — As Canada navigates new trade dynamics, embedded finance and payments innovation tied to automotive retail and consumer financing present emerging opportunities for Atlantic Canadian fintech players.

- **[With a crucial oil artery blocked near Iran, can Canada fill the gap in global supply?](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-fill-gap-supply-oil-energy-iran-9.7114328?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's potential role in stabilizing global energy supply amid the Iran conflict could drive increased capital flows and infrastructure investment into Atlantic Canada's offshore energy sector, with downstream implications for regional financial services and fintech.
